purpose: Empirical comparison of current YES Master preset render fingerprints against the existing BandLab fingerprint corpus. This file is evidence for future discussion, not an applied retune.

protocol_notes.yesmaster_render_protocol: YES Master Rust backend; input gain 0 dB; intensity 0.5; volume_match false; DeliveryProfile::Custom; target -14 LUFS; -1 dBTP ceiling; source sample rate and source bit depth for the 44.1 kHz/16-bit corpus.
protocol_notes.bandlab_protocol: Existing BandLab captures from the preset-research corpus; BandLab output loudness is whatever the service rendered for each preset.
protocol_notes.loudness_caveat: YES was deliberately held to -14 LUFS for this measurement. Loudness gaps are real output deltas under this protocol, but they are not by themselves proof that YES presets should be retuned louder.

distance_formula.summary: overall_character_distance is sqrt(sum(component^2)); it is a ranking heuristic, not an auditory truth metric.

components.eq_rmse_db: RMSE across mean-centered pink-noise EQ contour bands.
components.stereo_corr_gap_scaled: Absolute L/R correlation-change gap multiplied by 10.
components.stereo_width_gap_scaled: Absolute width-dB gap multiplied by 0.15 because width dB rides a near-mono floor.
components.click_lift_gap_scaled: Absolute sparse-click loudness-lift gap multiplied by 0.35.
components.tilt_gap_scaled: Absolute spectral tilt gap multiplied by 1.2.
components.density_gap_scaled: Absolute multiband density index gap multiplied by 3.

candidate_adjustment_directions: YES is 2.41 LU quieter than BandLab universal; if matching BandLab output behavior, consider a higher target loudness for this preset/protocol. candidate only; do not apply without owner listening signoff.
YES compresses less than BandLab by 3.99 dB on the dynamic test; candidate direction is more density/compression.
YES is narrower/less decorrelated than BandLab: correlation delta +0.006, width delta -2.01 dB; candidate direction is more stereo width/decorrelation.
YES pulls sparse clicks 3.03 LU harder; candidate direction is less transient/sparse-material lift if matching BandLab.
Tonal contour is lower in sub/low bass by -1.35 dB on average; candidate direction is more sub/low bass.
